g.data-narration: Welcome to Two-Dimensional Arrays in C. We'll explore declaration, initialization, and element access with visual examples.
A 2D array stores data in rows and columns like a matrix. Think of it as a spreadsheet or game board grid.
g.text: A 2D array stores elements in a tabular format with rows and columns.
Think of it like a matrix, spreadsheet, or game board grid.
Used for: Game boards, images (pixels), matrices, tabular data

g.data-narration: The syntax is data type array name with rows and columns in square brackets. Three examples show different data types.
2D arrays are stored contiguously in row-major order. Elements fill row zero first, then row one, and so on.
g.text: 2D arrays are stored contiguously in row-major order (row 0, then row 1, then row 2...)

g.data-narration: Use nested braces for clear row demarcation. Each row is enclosed in its own braces, making code readable and maintainable.
Unspecified elements automatically become zero. Perfect for initializing arrays with a default value of zero.
g.text: Unspecified elements are automatically set to zero.

g.data-narration: Use a single flat list. Elements fill row by row. Works but harder to read for larger arrays.
g.text: Single-level list filled row-by-row (less readable but valid).

g.data-narration: The compiler calculates rows from the initializer. Column dimension must always be specified, but row is optional.
g.text: Compiler deduces row count from initializer. Column dimension is mandatory!

g.data-narration: Access elements using array name with row and column indices. Both start from zero. Out of bounds causes undefined behavior.
g.text: Use two indices: array[row][column], both starting from 0.

g.data-narration: Use nested loops to process 2D arrays. Outer loop iterates rows, inner loop iterates columns for cache efficiency.
g.text: Use outer loop for rows, inner loop for columns (row-major order for cache efficiency).

g.data-narration: Understanding pointer arithmetic helps. Matrix points to first row, matrix i points to row i, and dereference gives elements.
Key points: column dimension required, row optional. Use row-major storage and nested braces for readability.
g.text: • Column dimension required, row optional | • Row-major memory storage | • Use nested braces for readability
• Partial init → zeros | • Process row-by-row for efficiency | • Index starts at 0

g.data-narration: Direct element access takes constant time O(1). Full traversal takes O(n times m).
